items.quote: The most exciting phrase to hear in science, the one that heralds new discoveries, is not 'Eureka!' but 'That's funny...'
items.author: Isaac Asimov

items.title: AcademiClaw: When Students Set Challenges for AI Agents
items.abstract: AcademiClaw introduces a new bilingual benchmark for evaluating AI agents on complex, long-horizon tasks derived directly from real university student academic workflows. It comprises 80 challenging problems spanning over 25 professional domains, designed to test capabilities beyond current assistant-level benchmarks. The evaluation uses isolated Docker sandboxes and multi-dimensional rubrics, offering a rigorous assessment of AI performance on demanding academic challenges.
items.figure_caption: Task complexity comparison: Claw-Eval vs. AcademiClaw. Claw-Eval focuses on assistant-level routines, whereas AcademiClaw targets tasks requiring deep academic expertise and sustained multi-step reasoning.

items.title: AI-Generated Smells: An Analysis of Code and Architecture in LLM and Agent-Driven Development
items.abstract: This paper systematically audits technical debt in AI-generated software, finding that LLMs introduce a distinct "machine signature" of defects rather than eliminating flaws. The core contribution is establishing a **Reasoning-Complexity Trade-off**, where more capable models produce increasingly bloated and coupled code, formalized by a **Volume-Quality Inverse Law**. This demonstrates that architectural decay persists regardless of functional correctness or prompting detail.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Distribution of Code Smell Counts. This box plot illustrates the distribution of counts for the most prevalent code smells, sorted in descending order by their mean value. Each box represents the interquartile range (IQR), with the central line denoting the median and the whiskers extending to 1.5 times the IQR. Points beyond the whiskers are plotted as individual outliers. The abbreviations for the code smells are as follows: TMB (Too Many Branches), PAU (Potential Improper API Usage), UD (Unstable Dependency), SF (Scattered Functionality), RFC (High Response for a Class), HCC (High Cyclomatic Complexity), TF (Temporal Field), and LCM (High Lack of Cohesion of Methods).

items.title: Foundation-Model-Based Agents in Industrial Automation: Purposes, Capabilities, and Open Challenges
items.abstract: This paper systematically surveys the literature to examine the current state, capabilities, and challenges of foundation-model-based agents in industrial automation. The core method involves a PRISMA-guided literature review synthesizing 88 relevant publications. The main contribution is revealing that most reported systems are still in early validation stages (TRL 4-6), primarily focusing on user assistance and monitoring rather than full deployment.

items.title: Mitigating Misalignment Contagion by Steering with Implicit Traits
items.abstract: This paper investigates "misalignment contagion," where language models (LMs) become more anti-social after multi-turn interactions, especially when interacting with malicious agents. The core contribution is proposing and demonstrating the effectiveness of **steering with implicit traits**—intermittently reinforcing an LM's initial system prompt—as a superior method to mitigate this contagion compared to static system prompt reinforcement.
items.figure_caption: The steps of our approach: (1) assign different personas to the language models (LMs) using default, benevolent or malicious system prompts, (2) conduct pre-game persona assessment and identify core implicit traits, (3) agents compete in multi-turn social dilemma games, (4) post-game assessment quantifies effects of misalignment contagion and our steering with implicit traits (SIT) intervention.

items.title: On Training Large Language Models for Long-Horizon Tasks: An Empirical Study of Horizon Length
items.abstract: This paper empirically investigates the impact of task horizon length on training Large Language Models (LLMs) for long-horizon tasks. The core method involves constructing controlled tasks where only the required action sequence length varies, keeping decision rules constant. The key contribution is demonstrating that increasing horizon length alone significantly hinders training stability due to exploration and credit assignment issues, suggesting horizon reduction as a crucial principle for improvement.
items.figure_caption: A summary of our contributions. In this work, we study the training of long-horizon LLM agents from a horizon-centric perspective and identify horizon length as a fundamental bottleneck. We show that horizon reduction stabilizes RL and strengthens the tendency toward horizon generalization on longer tasks with similar reasoning difficulty.

items.title: ORPilot: A Production-Oriented Agentic LLM-for-OR Tool for Optimization Modeling
items.abstract: ORPilot is an agentic LLM system designed to translate ambiguous, real-world business problems with raw data into solver-ready optimization models for production use. Its core method involves specialized agents for specification elicitation, independent data retrieval, and parameter computation, culminating in a solver-agnostic Intermediate Representation (IR). This contribution enables deterministic model recompilation across various commercial and open-source solvers, enhanced by self-correcting loops using solver tracebacks.
items.figure_caption: ORPilot standard pipeline. Blue indicates an LLM-involved step, while orange indicates a deterministic step. Double arrows in opposite directions indicate the interactive nature of this step. Solid arrows represent unconditional transitions between steps, while dashed arrows represent conditional transitions between steps.

items.title: Strategy-Aware Optimization Modeling with Reasoning LLMs
items.abstract: This paper introduces SAGE, a framework to improve Large Language Models' (LLMs) ability to select effective optimization modeling strategies. SAGE achieves this by explicitly incorporating modeling strategy into training using a solver-verified dataset and a novel Segment-Weighted GRPO fine-tuning process with a composite reward. This approach significantly boosts the LLM's success rate in generating correct and efficient optimization programs across various benchmarks.
items.figure_caption: Why modeling strategy matters. A step-wise pipeline may define variables on an incorrect index space (e.g., ( A , A ) (A,A) ), creating invalid arcs and runtime failures (e.g., KeyError ). Strategy-aware reasoning first commits to a paradigm (e.g., flow-based) and restricts the decision domain (e.g., Links ), producing a consistent and solver-executable model.

items.title: Beating the Style Detector: Three Hours of Agentic Research on the AI-Text Arms Race
items.abstract: This paper demonstrates the dramatic acceleration of NLP research using an agentic research harness, reproducing a complex study in just three hours. The core contribution is showing that state-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.7) can now close $71-75\%$ of the style gap in text post-editing compared to human edits. Furthermore, the work frames this capability within the context of an AI-text detection arms race, where current detectors remain highly effective.

items.title: Gradient-Gated DPO: Stabilizing Preference Optimization in Language Models
items.abstract: The paper introduces **Gradient-Gated Preference Optimization (Gate-DPO)** to stabilize Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) training, which suffers from a "squeezing effect" causing probability collapse. Gate-DPO achieves this by introducing a gating mechanism that attenuates the negative gradients applied to rejected responses when the model is already assigning them extremely low probability. This modulation prevents harmful gradient concentration, leading to more stable and effective preference alignment.
items.figure_caption: Empirical demonstration of three structural pathologies in DPO. All experiments use Pythia-410M on Anthropic-HH (5 epochs). (a) Unbounded optimization: after the SFT → \( \rightarrow \) DPO transition, absolute log-probabilities drift downward despite preference learning. (b) Squeezing: probability mass concentrates on the argmax while both chosen and rejected responses decrease. (c) Valley collapse: low-probability regions are disproportionately suppressed under standard DPO.

items.title: ContextualJailbreak: Evolutionary Red-Teaming via Simulated Conversational Priming
items.abstract: ContextualJailbreak introduces an evolutionary, black-box red-teaming strategy to automatically discover multi-turn jailbreak prompts that exploit LLM vulnerabilities via conversational priming. It searches over simulated dialogues, using a two-level harm scoring system to guide the evolution towards prompts that successfully elicit harmful responses, addressing the limitations of previous single-turn optimization methods.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1. End-to-end architecture of ContextualJailbreak . The pipeline generates contextual priming dialogues through an attacker model, tests them against a target LLM, and evaluates the responses via a two-stage judge system. Scored templates are then recycled to guide the ongoing evolutionary search.

items.title: Reinforcement Learning for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems through Orchestration Traces
items.abstract: This paper introduces **orchestration traces**—temporal interaction graphs—as a framework to apply reinforcement learning (RL) to coordinate teams of LLM agents. The core contribution lies in defining comprehensive **reward designs** that span eight families, including specific rewards for parallelism and aggregation quality, and analyzing how **credit signals** are assigned across various interaction units. This allows RL to optimize complex multi-agent behaviors beyond individual actions, focusing on decisions like spawning, delegation, and communication.

items.title: An Empirical Study of Agent Skills for Healthcare: Practice, Gaps, and Governance
items.abstract: This paper presents the first empirical analysis of agent skills for healthcare by examining 557 public skills, annotated across ten dimensions. The core finding is that existing public healthcare skills primarily focus on workflow automation and monitoring, rather than the diagnostic and treatment tasks emphasized in research, revealing significant gaps in coverage across the clinical lifecycle and specialized inputs. This work highlights that general technical risk assessment is insufficient for capturing unique clinical risks in agent deployment.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1. Distribution of healthcare skill size by token count (left) and file count (right).

items.title: Beyond State Machines: Executing Network Procedures with Agentic Tool-Calling Sequences
items.abstract: This paper explores using LLM-based AI agents to execute complex network procedures via sequences of tool calls, moving beyond traditional state machines. The authors investigate four different methods for procedure acquisition and execution distribution, evaluating them based on latency and correctness using a UE IP allocation case study. They find that approaches requiring extensive iterative agent-side reasoning lead to higher latency and more errors.
items.figure_caption: Comparison of four procedural execution approaches. (a) A1 embeds the procedure within the agent, (b) A2 retrieves the procedure from an external database, (c) A3 receives the procedure in the input prompt, and (d) A4 encapsulates the procedure within a single tool. The figure highlights the difference between iterative multi-step execution (A1–A3) and single-call execution (A4).

items.title: Compress Then Adapt? No, Do It Together via Task-aware Union of Subspaces
items.abstract: This paper introduces **JACTUS**, a novel framework that unifies model compression and parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) into a single process, overcoming the limitations of sequential methods. JACTUS estimates task-specific gradient covariances to form a **Task-aware Union of Subspaces**, within which it performs a projected low-rank approximation and globally allocates the parameter budget. This joint approach ensures the compressed subspace is optimally aligned with downstream task objectives.
items.figure_caption: Comparison of three paradigms: PEFT, compression then fine-tuning, and our joint adaptation and compression.

items.title: CoRAL: Contact-Rich Adaptive LLM-based Control for Robotic Manipulation
items.abstract: CoRAL is a modular framework that enables zero-shot control for contact-rich robotic manipulation by decoupling high-level reasoning from low-level control. It uses an LLM to synthesize context-aware objective functions that guide a sampling-based motion planner (MPPI), rather than acting as a direct controller. The system further integrates a VLM for initial semantic priors on physical dynamics, which are then refined in real-time through online system identification, bridging the gap between LLM reasoning and adaptive physical control.
items.figure_caption: Real-world execution of CoRAL across six different manipulation tasks.

items.title: Foundation Models to Unlock Real-World Evidence from Nationwide Medical Claims
items.abstract: This paper introduces **ReClaim**, a large-scale generative transformer foundation model trained on 43.8 billion medical events from nationwide claims data. ReClaim models complex, longitudinal healthcare trajectories involving diagnoses, procedures, medications, and costs. Its core contribution is demonstrating that this foundation model significantly outperforms existing specialized models across over 1,000 disease-onset prediction tasks, particularly benefiting predictions for rare diseases.
items.figure_caption: ReClaim framework and evaluation workflow. ( a ), Longitudinal medical events from patient claims are encoded as chronologically ordered trajectories, and the ReClaim foundation model autoregressively predicts future medical events including diagnoses, procedures, medications, and expenditure. ( b ), The study datasets comprise the MarketScan corpus partitioned into a final training set, a held-out internal test cohort for disease and expenditure prediction with retrospective and prospective temporal subsets, and two external testing datasets: EHRShot and Yale New Haven Health (YNHH). This partitioning and external sources enable four testing scenarios. ( c ), Transformer-based ReClaim models (Qwen3 architecture) are trained at three parameter scales (S: 140M, M: 700M, L: 1.7B) through next-token pre-training, followed for disease onset prediction by task-specific post-training. ( d ), ReClaim is evaluated on three downstream tasks: disease onset prediction for over 1,000 International Classification of Diseases, Tenth Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM) conditions, next-year healthcare expenditure forecasting, and RWE applications including propensity score modeling using ReClaim embeddings.

items.title: Hybrid Inspection and Task-Based Access Control in Zero-Trust Agentic AI
items.abstract: This paper introduces Continuous Agent Semantic Authorization (CASA), a hybrid runtime enforcement model to secure LLM-driven agents interacting with tools and resources. It employs a zero-trust interception layer combining five deterministic controls for structural integrity with a semantic inspection layer to validate tool call choices against the subject's original intent. The core contribution is enabling continuous, intent-aware authorization to mitigate risks from compromised or malicious agents in complex, multi-turn interactions.
items.figure_caption: An agentic application can exploit its intermediary position to hard-code tool calls, substitute tools, tamper with parameters, poison definitions, falsify returned data, or manipulate the LLM to invoke tools outside the intended task scope.

items.title: SpecKV: Adaptive Speculative Decoding with Compression-Aware Gamma Selection
items.abstract: SpecKV introduces a lightweight, adaptive controller to dynamically select the optimal speculation length ($\gamma$) for speculative decoding on-the-fly. This selection is based on signals extracted directly from the draft model, addressing the limitation of fixed $\gamma$ values. The core contribution is demonstrating that the optimal $\gamma$ varies significantly based on the target model's compression level, leading to improved efficiency over fixed-length speculation.

items.title: Trustworthy AI Suffers from Invariance Conflicts and Causality is The Solution
items.abstract: This paper argues that conflicts between trustworthy AI objectives (like fairness and robustness) stem from incompatible invariance requirements under different data-generating process changes. The core contribution is proposing **causality as a unifying framework** to understand, manage, and potentially resolve these trade-offs by guiding the selection of appropriate invariances. This perspective offers a path toward achieving multiple trustworthy AI goals simultaneously across various model types.
items.figure_caption: Causal resolution to trade-offs in trustworthy AI. See Appendix A for the details.

items.title: Visual Latents Know More Than They Say: Unsilencing Latent Reasoning in MLLMs
items.abstract: This paper introduces the "Silenced Visual Latents" phenomenon, where multimodal models suppress the useful reasoning encoded in their continuous visual latents in favor of direct visual input during training. To resolve this, the authors propose a method to "unsilence" these latents by freezing the backbone and optimizing the latent reasoning pathway independently at inference time using query-guided contrastive alignment. This approach leverages the richer, implicit reasoning within the visual latents for improved performance.
items.figure_caption: The joint loss landscape of Latent Visual Reasoning. Under joint optimization, the initial latents are simultaneously pulled toward two conflicting attractors: the autoregressive prediction objective (left) and the visual reasoning objective (right). The shortcut favored by the autoregressive prediction objective dissociates latent quality from latent effectiveness and bypasses meaningful latent reasoning, driving the latents toward a compromise state.

items.title: How OpenAI delivers low-latency voice AI at scale
items.summary: OpenAI details the technical strategies, including model optimization and infrastructure improvements, used to achieve low-latency, high-quality voice AI experiences at massive scale.

items.title: Google Chrome silently installs a 4 GB AI model on your device without consent
items.summary: Google Chrome is reportedly installing a large, four-gigabyte AI model onto user devices without explicit consent, raising privacy concerns.

items.title: Train Your Own LLM from Scratch
items.summary: This GitHub repository provides resources and code to guide users through the process of training their own Large Language Model (LLM) entirely from the ground up.

items.title: Securing a DoD contractor: Finding a multi-tenant authorization vulnerability
items.summary: Strix discovered and reported a critical multi-tenant authorization vulnerability in a Department of Defense (DoD) contractor's system, allowing unauthorized access to sensitive data.

items.title: Let's talk about LLMs
items.summary: The linked article discusses Large Language Models (LLMs), likely exploring their current state, capabilities, or future implications in technology.

items.title: OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft Back Bill to Fund 'AI Literacy' in Schools
items.summary: OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft are supporting a bipartisan bill aimed at funding "AI literacy" education within US schools.

items.title: Sierra Raises $950M at $15B Valuation
items.summary: AI platform Sierra raised $950 million in funding at a $15 billion valuation to enhance customer experiences.

items.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
items.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that allows users to interact with and utilize DeepSeek models directly within their command-line interface.

items.description: 🌊 The leading agent orchestration platform for Claude. Deploy intelligent multi-agent swarms, coordinate autonomous workflows, and build conversational AI systems. Features enterprise-grade architecture, self-learning swarm intelligence, RAG integration, and native Claude Code / Codex Integration
items.summary: ruflo is a leading agent orchestration platform for Claude, enabling the deployment of intelligent, self-learning multi-agent swarms for autonomous workflows, featuring enterprise architecture, RAG integration, and native Claude Code/Codex integration.

items.description: An autonomous agent for deep financial research
items.summary: Dexter is an autonomous agent designed for deep financial research, leveraging AI to analyze complex financial data and generate insights.

items.description: Open source DocuSign alternative. Create, fill, and sign digital documents ✍️
items.summary: DocuSeal is an open-source alternative to DocuSign, enabling users to create, fill out, and digitally sign electronic documents.

items.description: VSCode theme based off the easemate IDE and Jetbrains islands theme
items.summary: This VS Code theme, `vscode-dark-islands`, provides a dark color scheme inspired by the aesthetics of the Easemate IDE and JetBrains Islands theme.

items.description: Context window optimization for AI coding agents. Sandboxes tool output, 98% reduction. 14 platforms
items.summary: Context-Mode optimizes AI coding agent context windows by sandboxing tool output, achieving a 98% reduction across 14 platforms.

items.description: Incremental engine for long horizon agents 🌟 Star if you like it!
items.summary: Cocoindex is an incremental engine designed to efficiently manage and process information for long-horizon agents, enabling continuous, evolving knowledge updates.

items.description: A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables.
items.summary: This repository provides a complete, multi-agent AI agency where specialized agents, each with a distinct personality and expertise (like frontend wizards or Reddit ninjas), work together to deliver proven results.

items.description: A complete computer science study plan to become a software engineer.
items.summary: This repository provides a comprehensive computer science study plan, structured as a university curriculum, designed to equip individuals with the necessary knowledge for a software engineering career.

The core contribution is enabling continuous, intent-aware authorization to mitigate risks from compromised or malicious agents in complex, multi-turn interactions.
arxiv.figure_caption: An agentic application can exploit its intermediary position to hard-code tool calls, substitute tools, tamper with parameters, poison definitions, falsify returned data, or manipulate the LLM to invoke tools outside the intended task scope.

arxiv.title: SpecKV: Adaptive Speculative Decoding with Compression-Aware Gamma Selection
arxiv.abstract: SpecKV introduces a lightweight, adaptive controller to dynamically select the optimal speculation length ($\gamma$) for speculative decoding on-the-fly. This selection is based on signals extracted directly from the draft model, addressing the limitation of fixed $\gamma$ values. The core contribution is demonstrating that the optimal $\gamma$ varies significantly based on the target model's compression level, leading to improved efficiency over fixed-length speculation.

arxiv.title: Trustworthy AI Suffers from Invariance Conflicts and Causality is The Solution
arxiv.abstract: This paper argues that conflicts between trustworthy AI objectives (like fairness and robustness) stem from incompatible invariance requirements under different data-generating process changes. The core contribution is proposing **causality as a unifying framework** to understand, manage, and potentially resolve these trade-offs by guiding the selection of appropriate invariances. This perspective offers a path toward achieving multiple trustworthy AI goals simultaneously across various model types.
arxiv.figure_caption: Causal resolution to trade-offs in trustworthy AI. See Appendix A for the details.

arxiv.title: Visual Latents Know More Than They Say: Unsilencing Latent Reasoning in MLLMs
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces the "Silenced Visual Latents" phenomenon, where multimodal models suppress the useful reasoning encoded in their continuous visual latents in favor of direct visual input during training. To resolve this, the authors propose a method to "unsilence" these latents by freezing the backbone and optimizing the latent reasoning pathway independently at inference time using query-guided contrastive alignment. This approach leverages the richer, implicit reasoning within the visual latents for improved performance.
arxiv.figure_caption: The joint loss landscape of Latent Visual Reasoning. Under joint optimization, the initial latents are simultaneously pulled toward two conflicting attractors: the autoregressive prediction objective (left) and the visual reasoning objective (right). The shortcut favored by the autoregressive prediction objective dissociates latent quality from latent effectiveness and bypasses meaningful latent reasoning, driving the latents toward a compromise state.

papers.title: AcademiClaw: When Students Set Challenges for AI Agents
papers.abstract: AcademiClaw introduces a new bilingual benchmark for evaluating AI agents on complex, long-horizon tasks derived directly from real university student academic workflows. It comprises 80 challenging problems spanning over 25 professional domains, designed to test capabilities beyond current assistant-level benchmarks. The evaluation uses isolated Docker sandboxes and multi-dimensional rubrics, offering a rigorous assessment of AI performance on demanding academic challenges.
papers.figure_caption: Task complexity comparison: Claw-Eval vs. AcademiClaw. Claw-Eval focuses on assistant-level routines, whereas AcademiClaw targets tasks requiring deep academic expertise and sustained multi-step reasoning.

papers.title: AI-Generated Smells: An Analysis of Code and Architecture in LLM and Agent-Driven Development
papers.abstract: This paper systematically audits technical debt in AI-generated software, finding that LLMs introduce a distinct "machine signature" of defects rather than eliminating flaws. The core contribution is establishing a **Reasoning-Complexity Trade-off**, where more capable models produce increasingly bloated and coupled code, formalized by a **Volume-Quality Inverse Law**. This demonstrates that architectural decay persists regardless of functional correctness or prompting detail.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Distribution of Code Smell Counts. This box plot illustrates the distribution of counts for the most prevalent code smells, sorted in descending order by their mean value. Each box represents the interquartile range (IQR), with the central line denoting the median and the whiskers extending to 1.5 times the IQR. Points beyond the whiskers are plotted as individual outliers. The abbreviations for the code smells are as follows: TMB (Too Many Branches), PAU (Potential Improper API Usage), UD (Unstable Dependency), SF (Scattered Functionality), RFC (High Response for a Class), HCC (High Cyclomatic Complexity), TF (Temporal Field), and LCM (High Lack of Cohesion of Methods).

papers.title: Foundation-Model-Based Agents in Industrial Automation: Purposes, Capabilities, and Open Challenges
papers.abstract: This paper systematically surveys the literature to examine the current state, capabilities, and challenges of foundation-model-based agents in industrial automation. The core method involves a PRISMA-guided literature review synthesizing 88 relevant publications. The main contribution is revealing that most reported systems are still in early validation stages (TRL 4-6), primarily focusing on user assistance and monitoring rather than full deployment.

papers.title: Mitigating Misalignment Contagion by Steering with Implicit Traits
papers.abstract: This paper investigates "misalignment contagion," where language models (LMs) become more anti-social after multi-turn interactions, especially when interacting with malicious agents. The core contribution is proposing and demonstrating the effectiveness of **steering with implicit traits**—intermittently reinforcing an LM's initial system prompt—as a superior method to mitigate this contagion compared to static system prompt reinforcement.
papers.figure_caption: The steps of our approach: (1) assign different personas to the language models (LMs) using default, benevolent or malicious system prompts, (2) conduct pre-game persona assessment and identify core implicit traits, (3) agents compete in multi-turn social dilemma games, (4) post-game assessment quantifies effects of misalignment contagion and our steering with implicit traits (SIT) intervention.

papers.title: On Training Large Language Models for Long-Horizon Tasks: An Empirical Study of Horizon Length
papers.abstract: This paper empirically investigates the impact of task horizon length on training Large Language Models (LLMs) for long-horizon tasks. The core method involves constructing controlled tasks where only the required action sequence length varies, keeping decision rules constant. The key contribution is demonstrating that increasing horizon length alone significantly hinders training stability due to exploration and credit assignment issues, suggesting horizon reduction as a crucial principle for improvement.
papers.figure_caption: A summary of our contributions. In this work, we study the training of long-horizon LLM agents from a horizon-centric perspective and identify horizon length as a fundamental bottleneck. We show that horizon reduction stabilizes RL and strengthens the tendency toward horizon generalization on longer tasks with similar reasoning difficulty.

papers.title: ORPilot: A Production-Oriented Agentic LLM-for-OR Tool for Optimization Modeling
papers.abstract: ORPilot is an agentic LLM system designed to translate ambiguous, real-world business problems with raw data into solver-ready optimization models for production use. Its core method involves specialized agents for specification elicitation, independent data retrieval, and parameter computation, culminating in a solver-agnostic Intermediate Representation (IR). This contribution enables deterministic model recompilation across various commercial and open-source solvers, enhanced by self-correcting loops using solver tracebacks.
papers.figure_caption: ORPilot standard pipeline. Blue indicates an LLM-involved step, while orange indicates a deterministic step. Double arrows in opposite directions indicate the interactive nature of this step. Solid arrows represent unconditional transitions between steps, while dashed arrows represent conditional transitions between steps.

papers.title: Strategy-Aware Optimization Modeling with Reasoning LLMs
papers.abstract: This paper introduces SAGE, a framework to improve Large Language Models' (LLMs) ability to select effective optimization modeling strategies. SAGE achieves this by explicitly incorporating modeling strategy into training using a solver-verified dataset and a novel Segment-Weighted GRPO fine-tuning process with a composite reward. This approach significantly boosts the LLM's success rate in generating correct and efficient optimization programs across various benchmarks.
papers.figure_caption: Why modeling strategy matters. A step-wise pipeline may define variables on an incorrect index space (e.g., ( A , A ) (A,A) ), creating invalid arcs and runtime failures (e.g., KeyError ). Strategy-aware reasoning first commits to a paradigm (e.g., flow-based) and restricts the decision domain (e.g., Links ), producing a consistent and solver-executable model.

papers.title: Beating the Style Detector: Three Hours of Agentic Research on the AI-Text Arms Race
papers.abstract: This paper demonstrates the dramatic acceleration of NLP research using an agentic research harness, reproducing a complex study in just three hours. The core contribution is showing that state-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.7) can now close $71-75\%$ of the style gap in text post-editing compared to human edits. Furthermore, the work frames this capability within the context of an AI-text detection arms race, where current detectors remain highly effective.

papers.title: Gradient-Gated DPO: Stabilizing Preference Optimization in Language Models
papers.abstract: The paper introduces **Gradient-Gated Preference Optimization (Gate-DPO)** to stabilize Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) training, which suffers from a "squeezing effect" causing probability collapse. Gate-DPO achieves this by introducing a gating mechanism that attenuates the negative gradients applied to rejected responses when the model is already assigning them extremely low probability. This modulation prevents harmful gradient concentration, leading to more stable and effective preference alignment.
papers.figure_caption: Empirical demonstration of three structural pathologies in DPO. All experiments use Pythia-410M on Anthropic-HH (5 epochs). (a) Unbounded optimization: after the SFT → \( \rightarrow \) DPO transition, absolute log-probabilities drift downward despite preference learning. (b) Squeezing: probability mass concentrates on the argmax while both chosen and rejected responses decrease. (c) Valley collapse: low-probability regions are disproportionately suppressed under standard DPO.

papers.title: ContextualJailbreak: Evolutionary Red-Teaming via Simulated Conversational Priming
papers.abstract: ContextualJailbreak introduces an evolutionary, black-box red-teaming strategy to automatically discover multi-turn jailbreak prompts that exploit LLM vulnerabilities via conversational priming. It searches over simulated dialogues, using a two-level harm scoring system to guide the evolution towards prompts that successfully elicit harmful responses, addressing the limitations of previous single-turn optimization methods.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. End-to-end architecture of ContextualJailbreak . The pipeline generates contextual priming dialogues through an attacker model, tests them against a target LLM, and evaluates the responses via a two-stage judge system. Scored templates are then recycled to guide the ongoing evolutionary search.

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems through Orchestration Traces
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **orchestration traces**—temporal interaction graphs—as a framework to apply reinforcement learning (RL) to coordinate teams of LLM agents. The core contribution lies in defining comprehensive **reward designs** that span eight families, including specific rewards for parallelism and aggregation quality, and analyzing how **credit signals** are assigned across various interaction units. This allows RL to optimize complex multi-agent behaviors beyond individual actions, focusing on decisions like spawning, delegation, and communication.

papers.title: An Empirical Study of Agent Skills for Healthcare: Practice, Gaps, and Governance
papers.abstract: This paper presents the first empirical analysis of agent skills for healthcare by examining 557 public skills, annotated across ten dimensions. The core finding is that existing public healthcare skills primarily focus on workflow automation and monitoring, rather than the diagnostic and treatment tasks emphasized in research, revealing significant gaps in coverage across the clinical lifecycle and specialized inputs. This work highlights that general technical risk assessment is insufficient for capturing unique clinical risks in agent deployment.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. Distribution of healthcare skill size by token count (left) and file count (right).

papers.title: Beyond State Machines: Executing Network Procedures with Agentic Tool-Calling Sequences
papers.abstract: This paper explores using LLM-based AI agents to execute complex network procedures via sequences of tool calls, moving beyond traditional state machines. The authors investigate four different methods for procedure acquisition and execution distribution, evaluating them based on latency and correctness using a UE IP allocation case study. They find that approaches requiring extensive iterative agent-side reasoning lead to higher latency and more errors.
papers.figure_caption: Comparison of four procedural execution approaches. (a) A1 embeds the procedure within the agent, (b) A2 retrieves the procedure from an external database, (c) A3 receives the procedure in the input prompt, and (d) A4 encapsulates the procedure within a single tool. The figure highlights the difference between iterative multi-step execution (A1–A3) and single-call execution (A4).

papers.title: Compress Then Adapt? No, Do It Together via Task-aware Union of Subspaces
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **JACTUS**, a novel framework that unifies model compression and parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) into a single process, overcoming the limitations of sequential methods. JACTUS estimates task-specific gradient covariances to form a **Task-aware Union of Subspaces**, within which it performs a projected low-rank approximation and globally allocates the parameter budget. This joint approach ensures the compressed subspace is optimally aligned with downstream task objectives.
papers.figure_caption: Comparison of three paradigms: PEFT, compression then fine-tuning, and our joint adaptation and compression.

papers.title: CoRAL: Contact-Rich Adaptive LLM-based Control for Robotic Manipulation
papers.abstract: CoRAL is a modular framework that enables zero-shot control for contact-rich robotic manipulation by decoupling high-level reasoning from low-level control. It uses an LLM to synthesize context-aware objective functions that guide a sampling-based motion planner (MPPI), rather than acting as a direct controller. The system further integrates a VLM for initial semantic priors on physical dynamics, which are then refined in real-time through online system identification, bridging the gap between LLM reasoning and adaptive physical control.
papers.figure_caption: Real-world execution of CoRAL across six different manipulation tasks.

papers.title: Foundation Models to Unlock Real-World Evidence from Nationwide Medical Claims
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **ReClaim**, a large-scale generative transformer foundation model trained on 43.8 billion medical events from nationwide claims data. ReClaim models complex, longitudinal healthcare trajectories involving diagnoses, procedures, medications, and costs. Its core contribution is demonstrating that this foundation model significantly outperforms existing specialized models across over 1,000 disease-onset prediction tasks, particularly benefiting predictions for rare diseases.
papers.figure_caption: ReClaim framework and evaluation workflow. ( a ), Longitudinal medical events from patient claims are encoded as chronologically ordered trajectories, and the ReClaim foundation model autoregressively predicts future medical events including diagnoses, procedures, medications, and expenditure. ( b ), The study datasets comprise the MarketScan corpus partitioned into a final training set, a held-out internal test cohort for disease and expenditure prediction with retrospective and prospective temporal subsets, and two external testing datasets: EHRShot and Yale New Haven Health (YNHH). This partitioning and external sources enable four testing scenarios. ( c ), Transformer-based ReClaim models (Qwen3 architecture) are trained at three parameter scales (S: 140M, M: 700M, L: 1.7B) through next-token pre-training, followed for disease onset prediction by task-specific post-training. ( d ), ReClaim is evaluated on three downstream tasks: disease onset prediction for over 1,000 International Classification of Diseases, Tenth Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-10-CM) conditions, next-year healthcare expenditure forecasting, and RWE applications including propensity score modeling using ReClaim embeddings.

papers.title: Hybrid Inspection and Task-Based Access Control in Zero-Trust Agentic AI
papers.abstract: This paper introduces Continuous Agent Semantic Authorization (CASA), a hybrid runtime enforcement model to secure LLM-driven agents interacting with tools and resources. It employs a zero-trust interception layer combining five deterministic controls for structural integrity with a semantic inspection layer to validate tool call choices against the subject's original intent. The core contribution is enabling continuous, intent-aware authorization to mitigate risks from compromised or malicious agents in complex, multi-turn interactions.
papers.figure_caption: An agentic application can exploit its intermediary position to hard-code tool calls, substitute tools, tamper with parameters, poison definitions, falsify returned data, or manipulate the LLM to invoke tools outside the intended task scope.

papers.title: SpecKV: Adaptive Speculative Decoding with Compression-Aware Gamma Selection
papers.abstract: SpecKV introduces a lightweight, adaptive controller to dynamically select the optimal speculation length ($\gamma$) for speculative decoding on-the-fly. This selection is based on signals extracted directly from the draft model, addressing the limitation of fixed $\gamma$ values. The core contribution is demonstrating that the optimal $\gamma$ varies significantly based on the target model's compression level, leading to improved efficiency over fixed-length speculation.

papers.title: Trustworthy AI Suffers from Invariance Conflicts and Causality is The Solution
papers.abstract: This paper argues that conflicts between trustworthy AI objectives (like fairness and robustness) stem from incompatible invariance requirements under different data-generating process changes. The core contribution is proposing **causality as a unifying framework** to understand, manage, and potentially resolve these trade-offs by guiding the selection of appropriate invariances. This perspective offers a path toward achieving multiple trustworthy AI goals simultaneously across various model types.
papers.figure_caption: Causal resolution to trade-offs in trustworthy AI. See Appendix A for the details.

papers.title: Visual Latents Know More Than They Say: Unsilencing Latent Reasoning in MLLMs
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the "Silenced Visual Latents" phenomenon, where multimodal models suppress the useful reasoning encoded in their continuous visual latents in favor of direct visual input during training. To resolve this, the authors propose a method to "unsilence" these latents by freezing the backbone and optimizing the latent reasoning pathway independently at inference time using query-guided contrastive alignment. This approach leverages the richer, implicit reasoning within the visual latents for improved performance.
papers.figure_caption: The joint loss landscape of Latent Visual Reasoning. Under joint optimization, the initial latents are simultaneously pulled toward two conflicting attractors: the autoregressive prediction objective (left) and the visual reasoning objective (right). The shortcut favored by the autoregressive prediction objective dissociates latent quality from latent effectiveness and bypasses meaningful latent reasoning, driving the latents toward a compromise state.

hacker_news.title: How OpenAI delivers low-latency voice AI at scale
hacker_news.summary: OpenAI details the technical strategies, including model optimization and infrastructure improvements, used to achieve low-latency, high-quality voice AI experiences at massive scale.

hacker_news.title: Google Chrome silently installs a 4 GB AI model on your device without consent
hacker_news.summary: Google Chrome is reportedly installing a large, four-gigabyte AI model onto user devices without explicit consent, raising privacy concerns.

hacker_news.title: Train Your Own LLM from Scratch
hacker_news.summary: This GitHub repository provides resources and code to guide users through the process of training their own Large Language Model (LLM) entirely from the ground up.

hacker_news.title: Securing a DoD contractor: Finding a multi-tenant authorization vulnerability
hacker_news.summary: Strix discovered and reported a critical multi-tenant authorization vulnerability in a Department of Defense (DoD) contractor's system, allowing unauthorized access to sensitive data.

hacker_news.title: Let's talk about LLMs
hacker_news.summary: The linked article discusses Large Language Models (LLMs), likely exploring their current state, capabilities, or future implications in technology.

hacker_news.title: OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft Back Bill to Fund 'AI Literacy' in Schools
hacker_news.summary: OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft are supporting a bipartisan bill aimed at funding "AI literacy" education within US schools.

hacker_news.title: Sierra Raises $950M at $15B Valuation
hacker_news.summary: AI platform Sierra raised $950 million in funding at a $15 billion valuation to enhance customer experiences.

github_trending.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
github_trending.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that allows users to interact with and utilize DeepSeek models directly within their command-line interface.

github_trending.description: 🌊 The leading agent orchestration platform for Claude. Deploy intelligent multi-agent swarms, coordinate autonomous workflows, and build conversational AI systems. Features enterprise-grade architecture, self-learning swarm intelligence, RAG integration, and native Claude Code / Codex Integration
github_trending.summary: ruflo is a leading agent orchestration platform for Claude, enabling the deployment of intelligent, self-learning multi-agent swarms for autonomous workflows, featuring enterprise architecture, RAG integration, and native Claude Code/Codex integration.

github_trending.description: An autonomous agent for deep financial research
github_trending.summary: Dexter is an autonomous agent designed for deep financial research, leveraging AI to analyze complex financial data and generate insights.

github_trending.description: Open source DocuSign alternative. Create, fill, and sign digital documents ✍️
github_trending.summary: DocuSeal is an open-source alternative to DocuSign, enabling users to create, fill out, and digitally sign electronic documents.

github_trending.description: VSCode theme based off the easemate IDE and Jetbrains islands theme
github_trending.summary: This VS Code theme, `vscode-dark-islands`, provides a dark color scheme inspired by the aesthetics of the Easemate IDE and JetBrains Islands theme.

github_trending.description: Context window optimization for AI coding agents. Sandboxes tool output, 98% reduction. 14 platforms
github_trending.summary: Context-Mode optimizes AI coding agent context windows by sandboxing tool output, achieving a 98% reduction across 14 platforms.

github_trending.description: Incremental engine for long horizon agents 🌟 Star if you like it!
github_trending.summary: Cocoindex is an incremental engine designed to efficiently manage and process information for long-horizon agents, enabling continuous, evolving knowledge updates.

github_trending.description: A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a complete, multi-agent AI agency where specialized agents, each with a distinct personality and expertise (like frontend wizards or Reddit ninjas), work together to deliver proven results.

github_trending.description: A complete computer science study plan to become a software engineer.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a comprehensive computer science study plan, structured as a university curriculum, designed to equip individuals with the necessary knowledge for a software engineering career.
